introduction: Stye could be external or internal. External stye is the infection of the lash follicle and its associated gland of Zeis or Moll while internal stye (chalazion) is the infection of the meibomian gland.

features: Painful lump growing on the eyelid
Red swollen area on the eyelid (like a boil)
Pain in the affected area of the eyelid
Firm, painless lump on the eyelid, usually upper lid

non_drug: Apply warm wet pads for 15 minutes 4 times daily until the stye drains
Incision and curettage (if there is still a chalazion lump) as soon as the infection settles
drug: Antibiotic eye ointment to stop infection
Chloramphenicol ointment apply 4 times daily for 2 weeks
Amoxicillin 250 - 500 mg orally every 8 hours for 5 - 7 days
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Discourage the use of traditional eye medication

prevention: For recurrent styes, use baby shampoo to clean the eyelashes regularly
